description: NextEra Energy is a U.S. electric utility and energy infrastructure company anchored by Florida Power & Light and a large competitive generation platform focused on renewables, storage, nuclear, and contracted power assets.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: NextEra Energy operates through two main engines: Florida Power & Light, a large regulated electric utility serving Florida, and NextEra Energy Resources, a competitive energy infrastructure business with generation, storage, and customer-supply assets across North America.

The company presents itself as both a traditional utility holding company and a clean-energy growth platform. That dual identity is strategically useful: regulated wires and rate cases provide durable cash flow, while renewables and storage provide the future-facing story investors are politely expected to applaud.

overview.title: Core Products and Services
paragraphs: Florida Power & Light delivers electricity to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Florida, supported by generation assets, transmission and distribution infrastructure, substations, and customer-service operations.

NextEra Energy Resources develops, owns, and operates energy projects including wind, solar, battery storage, nuclear, natural gas, and related infrastructure. Its business model relies heavily on long-term contracts, large-scale project development, and the ability to finance and interconnect assets at industrial scale.

overview.title: Registry Relevance
paragraphs: For Free The World, NextEra belongs in the utilities and electric-utilities category because it controls essential electricity delivery and generation infrastructure rather than a consumer software platform or hardware ecosystem.

The most relevant alternative mapping is likely to involve community energy, public-power models, grid transparency, distributed solar, storage, demand response, and open energy-data initiatives. The company is not yet mapped to a specific technology wave in repo context, which is appropriate until the registry defines a utility-sector wave with enough precision.
